signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.7% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate rose to 2.7% in March 2026, up from 1.9% in February and confirming preliminary estimates; this is the highest since January 2024, driven by a 7.2% surge in energy prices (fuel +20%, heating oil +44.4%) amid the Iran war.[2][3]  
The increase exceeds February's rate and aligns with forecasts,
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation at 2.2%, Below Forecasts | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, but missed market expectations of 2.4% while staying near the Bank of Korea's 2% target.[1]

signals.headline: US Blockade of Iranian Ports Begins Despite Ceasefire | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Blockade of Iranian Ports Begins Despite Ceasefire | YES | HIGH confidence**
The US military initiated a blockade of Iranian ports on April 13, 2026, during an ongoing ceasefire[1]. President Trump threatened to "immediately ELIMINATE" any Iranian ships approaching the blockade[1], stating that Iran is "doing absolutely no business" and that the US will "keep it that way"[2].

signals.headline: Islamabad Negotiations Collapsed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Islamabad Negotiations Collapsed | YES | HIGH confidence**
US-led talks in Islamabad ended without progress on April 12, 2026[6]. The breakdown centered on three key issues: Iran's uranium enrichment, funding for regional proxy groups (Hamas and Hezbollah), and reopening the Strait of Hormuz without Iranian tolls[1].
